message:
feat(startup): add getprogname + setprogname (BSD) (bd-zt2w1)

NetBSD/OpenBSD-origin program-name accessors (also exposed by
glibc 2.41+). Used by tools and libraries that want to query or
override the basename of argv[0] without depending on the
GNU-specific program_invocation_short_name global. Currently
missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi.

* getprogname() returns the cached short name, or a static empty
  C string if startup hasn't yet published one (never NULL).
* setprogname(name) walks `name` to its basename (last "/" + 1)
  and stores that pointer in the same AtomicPtr that
  program_invocation_short_name and __progname already use — so
  the BSD, GNU, and __progname vocabularies stay in sync. The
  caller retains ownership of the buffer per NetBSD setprogname(3).
* NULL setprogname is a no-op, matching NetBSD; the previously
  cached pointer survives.

7 abi integration tests covering: empty-fallback when slot is
NULL, basename extraction with absolute path, no-slash bare name,
trailing-slash → empty, NULL-pointer no-op, override semantics,
pointer aliasing with program_invocation_short_name. Tests share
STARTUP_TEST_LOCK with the existing phase0 tests so they don't
race the same globals. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
